brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +53.6% since Feb 27 — Brent $109.03 (Apr 2 close); markets closed Apr 3-5 (Good Friday/weekend); April 6 ultimatum <24 hours; WSO rescued; ceasefire 2%; Moody's recession 49%
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 2 close: $109.03/bbl. Markets closed Apr 3-5 (Good Friday/weekend). Brent range on Apr 2: $97.50-$113.97. Peak $127.61 during conflict. March +~55% — largest monthly gain since inception in 1988. April 6 Hormuz ultimatum <24 hours — Trump threatening civilian infrastructure. WSO rescued Apr 5 after 48+ hours inside Iran. Ceasefire probability 2% by Apr 7. IEA: 'April will be much worse than March.' Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs / IEA.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +68.5% since Feb 27 — WTI $112.06 (Apr 2 settle); first close above $100 since Jul 2022; WTI-Brent spread inverted; markets closed Apr 3-5; Bloomberg: dual chokepoint could push oil to $140
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 2 settle: $112.06/bbl. First close above $100 since July 2022. WTI-Brent spread inverted — reflecting acute US domestic supply anxiety. Gas at $4.104/gal nationally (Apr 5 AAA). Diesel $5.43/gal. California $5.89/gal. Markets closed Apr 3-5. Bloomberg Economics: dual chokepoint (Hormuz + Bab al-Mandeb) could push oil to $140/bbl. IEA: 'April will be much worse than March' as pre-war cargoes exhausted. Apr 6 energy deadline <24 hours. Source: NYMEX / AAA / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs / IEA.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.124 / +37.7% since Feb 27 — $4.104 nationally (Apr 5 AAA) — CA $5.89 — crossed $4 for first time since Aug 2022 — largest monthly jump on record
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Apr 5: $4.104/gal nationally (AAA) — $1.124 surge in 37 days. Crossed $4 for first time since August 2022. California $5.89/gal. Up from $2.98 on Feb 26. Goldman: every $10/bbl crude increase adds ~$0.25/gal at pump. Monthly jump exceeds steepest climbs after Hurricane Katrina (2005) and Russia-Ukraine invasion (2022). Source: AAA / EIA / Goldman Sachs.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -296 pts / -4.3% — S&P 6,582.69 (Apr 2 close); markets closed Apr 3-5; first weekly gain since war (+3.4%); jobs +178K (3x expected); Moody's recession 49%; VIX 23.87; CPI Apr 10
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Apr 2 close: 6,582.69 (+0.11%). Markets closed Apr 3-5 (Good Friday/weekend). First weekly gain since conflict began (+3.4%). Dow 46,504.67. Nasdaq 21,879.18 (entered correction -10% in late March). VIX 23.87. Jobs: +178K vs 57-65K expected; Feb revised down to -133K. Unemployment 4.3%. Moody's recession probability 49% — one point below threshold that preceded every US recession in 80 years. Goldman 30%, EY-Parthenon 40%. March CPI release Apr 10 expected to show significant acceleration from 2.4%. CME FedWatch: >50% rate hike probability — stagflation trap. Gold $4,702.70/oz. 10Y 4.31%. Source: NYSE / Bloomberg / CNBC / BLS / Moody's.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$1.0-1.5B/day — WSO rescued after 48+ hrs inside Iran — CIA deception op — 82nd Airborne deploying — April 6 ultimatum <24 hours — daily costs $890M-$2B
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 37: ~$1.0-1.5B/day (range $890M-$2B). WSO rescued Apr 5 after 48+ hours evading IRGC — extraordinary operation involving CIA deception campaign and makeshift airbase inside Iran. 82nd Airborne deploying 2,000-3,000 paratroopers. 6,000-8,000 ground troops positioning near Iran. Three CSGs committed: Lincoln (Arabian Sea), Ford (Eastern Med), Bush (Atlantic transit). April 6 energy deadline <24 hours — Trump threatening civilian infrastructure. Pentagon $200B+ supplemental not yet submitted. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / CENTCOM / USNI / Reuters.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (37 Days)
total_cost.note: ~$44.3B through Day 37 — $200B+ supplemental not submitted — Boebert (R-CO) 'a no' — Murkowski demands plan — daily costs $890M-$2B — Trump approval 35% (CNN) / 33% (UMass) — 61% wrong decision (Pew)
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 37: ~$44.3B. Penn Wharton Budget Model: $40B-$95B in direct costs, up to $210B total economic impact. $200B+ supplemental not formally submitted. Rep. Boebert (R-CO) declared 'a no'; Sen. Murkowski demands strategic plan. Trump approval: CNN/SSRS 35% / UMass 33% / Silver Bulletin 39.7% — new second-term low. War-specific: 37% approve (Pew), 61% wrong decision. Even Fox News: 58% opposition. AG Pam Bondi fired Apr 3. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / Penn Wharton / CNN / Pew / Fox News.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CENTCOM: 12,300+ military targets across 13,000+ missions — Adm. Cooper: 'no navy sailing, no aircraft flying, air defenses largely destroyed' — 155+ warships/subs destroyed (~92% of Iran's fleet)
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM (as of Apr 1): 12,300+ military targets across 13,000+ missions. Adm. Brad Cooper: 'We don't see their navy sailing. We don't see their aircraft flying, and their air and missile defense systems have largely been destroyed.' Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~35,000+ through Day 37. 155+ warships and submarines destroyed (~92% of Iran's large warships). However, Iran retains ~50% of missile launchers and 1,000+ ballistic missiles per IDF. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Pentagon / CSIS.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 15 US KIA (13 combat, 1 health-related, 1 non-hostile + 6 KC-135) — WSO rescued Apr 5 after 48+ hours inside Iran — 7 aircraft lost + 12+ MQ-9 drones
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 15 KIA: 13 combat, 1 health-related, 1 non-hostile death; plus 6 in KC-135 crash categorized separately by some sources. F-15E WSO rescued Apr 5 after 48+ hours evading IRGC — CIA deception campaign. Total aircraft losses: 7 airframes (3 F-15Es Kuwait friendly fire Mar 2; 1 KC-135 Mar 12; 1 F-15E shot down Apr 3; 1 A-10 Apr 3) + 12+ MQ-9 Reaper drones. Key incidents: 6 KIA Camp Arifjan Kuwait (Mar 1); 6 KIA KC-135 crash Iraq (Mar 12). Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / White House / Stars and Stripes.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 365 WIA (Pentagon end of week 5) — The Intercept: 520+ including smoke inhalation — 'casualty cover-up' — SecDef Hegseth fired 12+ senior leaders including Army Chief Gen. George
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. Pentagon (end of week 5): 365 WIA. The Intercept reports 520+ including smoke inhalation incidents, accusing Pentagon of 'casualty cover-up.' SecDef Hegseth fired Army Chief Gen. Randy George — 12+ senior military leaders purged since taking office. Five former defense secretaries condemned the pattern as 'reckless.' Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / The Intercept / Reuters.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Health Min: 2,076+ killed, 26,500+ wounded — HRANA: 3,519+ (1,607 civilians, 244+ children) — Hengaw: 7,300+ (890 civilian, 6,410 military) — 3.2M displaced — ~20,000 civilian buildings affected
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — dramatic discrepancies across sources reflecting fog of war and internet blackout. Iran Health Ministry: 2,076+ killed, 26,500+ wounded. HRANA: 3,519+ (1,607 civilians, 244+ children). Hengaw (Kurdish rights): 7,300+ (890 civilians, 6,410 military in 34 days). Iran International: 4,700+ security forces. Combined extrapolated estimate: ~8,500+. Iran retains ~50% of missile launchers and 1,000+ ballistic missiles. ~20,000 civilian buildings affected. 307 health/medical facilities damaged. 3.2 million displaced. IAEA severed since Feb 26. Source: Iran Health Ministry / HRANA / Hengaw / Iran International / OCHA / IAEA.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,345+ killed — 4,040+ wounded — ~1.2M displaced (1/6 of pop.) — 189,000+ into Syria — 370,000+ children affected — IDF Sgt. Guy Ludar killed Apr 4 (friendly fire)
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli strikes and ground invasion since March 2. 1,345+ killed (Al Jazeera/Lebanese MoH). 4,040+ wounded. UNICEF: 121+ children killed, 399 injured. ~1.2 million displaced (1/6 of population). 189,000+ crossed into Syria. 370,000+ children affected. IDF Sgt. First Class Guy Ludar, 21, killed in Lebanon Apr 4, reportedly by friendly fire. Hezbollah rockets reaching 60km into Israel (Umm al-Fahm). Hezbollah claimed missile strike against Israeli warship Apr 5. Israel ordered evacuation of Masnaa crossing with Syria. IDF expanding toward Litani River. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / UNICEF / Al Jazeera / IDF / UNHCR.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: UNHCR: 7.2M+ displaced — Iran 3.2M (incl. ~864,000 children) — additional 4M across region (Lebanon, Iraq, Jordan) — Lebanon 1.2M (1/6 of pop.) — 115M need humanitarian aid across Eastern Med
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. UNHCR estimates 3.2 million internally displaced in Iran (including ~864,000 children). An additional 4 million displaced across the region (Lebanon, Iraq, Jordan). Total: 7.2M+. Lebanon: 1.2 million displaced (1/6 of population), with 189,000+ crossing into Syria and 370,000+ children affected. 115 million people across Eastern Mediterranean require humanitarian assistance. WHO $30.3M flash appeal (Mar-Aug 2026). 20+ verified attacks on Iranian healthcare (WHO). Source: UNHCR / WHO / OCHA / UNICEF.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — ~3,200 vessels stranded — Hormuz 6-12 transits/day (93% collapse) — only ~150 total since Mar 1 — Bloomberg 7-day average at war high — Apr 6 deadline <24 hrs
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. ~3,200 ships (~4% global tonnage) idle, 500+ waiting outside. Hormuz 6-12 transits/day, down 93% from 130-138 pre-war. Only ~150 total vessels since Mar 1. Bloomberg 7-day rolling average at war high as of Apr 4. March crude loadings fell 76%. War-risk premiums 1.5-3% of hull value ($10-14M per VLCC). IRGC charging $1/barrel in yuan/stablecoins. Source: IMO / IATA / Lloyd's / Bloomberg / IEA.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: HRANA: 244+ children (Iran) + UNICEF: 121+ (Lebanon) + Israel 4 + Kuwait 1 — Minab school (165-180 killed) — Pentagon: 'targeting error rooted in stale intelligence' — UN: potential war crime
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. HRANA: 244+ children killed in Iran as of April 4. UNICEF: 121+ children killed in Lebanon, 399 injured. Israel: 4. Kuwait: 1. Deadliest single incident: Minab girls' school (Feb 28) — 165-180 killed, mostly girls aged 7-12. Pentagon acknowledged US was 'likely responsible' due to 'targeting error rooted in stale intelligence data.' UN characterized it as potential war crime. IRGC child soldier recruitment (children as young as 12) condemned by Amnesty International and HRW. 'Janfada' volunteer campaign claims 7M registrants. Source: UNICEF / HRANA / Lebanese MoH / UNHRC / Amnesty International.
